title: SAP Q3 2025 Interim Report
content: WALLDORF, Germany — SAP SE today announced third-quarter 2025 results. Cloud revenue increased 27% (26% at constant currencies) to €4.35 billion. Cloud ERP Suite — SAP's most strategic cloud offering — grew 34% to €3.89 billion. Current cloud backlog (CCB), a forward-looking metric, grew 25% to €15.4 billion, indicating strong future contracted cloud revenue. Total revenue was €8.48 billion, up 10%. Software licenses revenue declined to €0.24 billion as SAP accelerates the transition from perpetual licensing to cloud subscriptions. Operating profit (IFRS) was €1.47 billion; adjusted operating profit was €2.66 billion, a margin of 31.4%. SAP raised its 2025 full-year guidance: cloud revenue €17.0-17.3 billion (up from €17.0-17.2 billion), and total revenue €29.8-30.2 billion. Christian Klein, CEO, said: 'We are moving with great speed to make SAP the AI company for business. Our Joule copilot and Business AI are now embedded across every major product line, driving strong cloud adoption.' SAP employs approximately 107,000 people globally and serves 280,000 customers in 190 countries. The Walldorf-based company has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 1998 and is a component of the DAX 40 index.
